intent:
At the station, a stranger walks away leaving her umbrella behind on the path; the narrator notices, recognizes whose umbrella it is, and picks it up. A small kindness across the boundary of family.

NATURALNESS REWRITE 2026-04-30: This is the second authoring of story 5. The first ship had five of seven sentences flagged by the §E.7.5 native-naturalness reviewer (wrong particles, 持つ overloaded as take/receive/remove, bare-quoted dialogue with no verb-of-saying frame, 持ちません vs 持っていません). This rewrite is built around verbs that ACTUALLY mean what the scene needs: 取る (pick up — N5) for the closer instead of 持つ. No dialogue is staged because the only N5 framing 「…」と言いました would tag と as companion-と and trip lint 11.4 with the quoted clause as inanimate; rather than burn an override, the narrator's act of kindness is shown in pure action with the woman absent at the close. Two new grammar points (G024_da plain copula and G034_ne_confirm) land in narrator's internal-thought sentences, which is the canonical home of the plain copula in Japanese prose.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 5)
  one_sentence_event:    A woman at the station walks away leaving her umbrella behind, and I pick it up off the path.
  change_of_state:       at s2 the umbrella is in the woman's possession; at s3 she walks away from it; at s4 it sits unclaimed on the path; at the closer it is in the narrator's hand, taken up to be returned. The world has materially moved: an object was about to be lost and a stranger child saved it.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a {classroom, kitchen, walk_to_shop} story because the last 3 were. Scene is station — first appearance in the v2 corpus.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 傘 (umbrella). Last 5 anchors were apple, egg, pencil, name-on-paper, tea_cup — umbrella is the first weather-implement anchor.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: noun-WA-noun-WO-verb-MASHITA (私は傘を取りました). Distinct from all banned shapes — short, action-driven, narrator as subject of a kind act.
  why_not_filler:        私 — narrator AND the hand that picks up the umbrella; the moral act is theirs alone. 母 — scene-grounding companion (s0/s1 establish the wait). 女の人 — the stranger whose forgetfulness creates the moment; her departure is the inciting incident. 傘 — the anchor; it changes possession-state across three sentences (held → unowned → narrator's hand) and triggers the narrator's kind act.
  obligations_absorbed:  Two new N5 grammar points: G034_ne_confirm (s4 ね — narrator confirms to themselves the umbrella's location) and G024_da (s5 だ — narrator's plain-copula realization of whose umbrella it is). G013_mashita_past reinforced via the closer 取りました. G008_te_iru reinforced via s2 持っています. G004_ni_location reinforced via s0/s4. G010_to_and reinforced via s0/s1 (companion-と). G017_de_means and G036_masen are intro'd in stories 3 and 4 and have no must-reinforce obligation here per the relaxed R1 rule.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY (from forbid.py — first authoring):
  scene_class    (last 3): classroom, kitchen, walk_to_shop
  anchor_object  (last 5): apple, egg, pencil, name, tea_cup
  closer_shape   (last 3): noun-WA-noun, noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun, noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-WO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3): 朝/母/私; 朝/台所/卵; 私/学校/います

All honored: scene=station; anchor=umbrella; closer is noun-WA-noun-WO-verb-MASHITA (different from banned shapes); opening 私/母/駅 is fresh.

MINT BUDGET: 7 mints exactly (cap 4-7). Mints: 駅 (s0), 電車 (s1), 待つ (s1), 女 (s2), 人 (s2), 傘 (s2), 取る (s6). All N5, no overrides.

NATURALNESS NOTES: Each sentence was vetted against §E.7.5. s2 uses 持っています for the stative 'is holding' (correct te-iru, not the broken bare-で-stative of the first ship). s5 introduces the plain copula だ inside the narrator's internal recognition — a genuinely native pattern (Japanese narrators routinely shift to plain form for inner thought). s6 uses 取る ('pick up'), the actually-correct verb for the closing action — first ship used 持つ and the reviewer correctly flagged it as wrong-verb (持つ means 'hold/carry as ongoing state', not 'pick up').

sentences.en: I am at the station with mother.

sentences.jp: 私は母と電車を待ちます。
sentences.en: I wait for the train with mother.

sentences.jp: 女の人は傘を持っています。
sentences.en: A woman is holding an umbrella.
The woman is no longer there.
